markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define receipts another model needs to resume after compaction or crash. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: CrdtRecoveryReceiptFormat leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CrdtRecoveryReceiptFormat is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 bac0570d; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: RECOVERY SCOPE GAP (high) -- CONFIRMED the implementer's own honest-risk note. recover_from_checkpoint (recovery_receipt.rs:161-261) verifies only that the new lease EXISTS: 'if get_lease(new_lease_id).is_none() -> NewLeaseNotFound' (line 190-197). It does NOT check that the new lease's scope_ref covers checkpoint.scope_ref. A new lease scoped to document:OTHER (or workspace:X) successfully recovers a checkpoint scoped to document:TARGET, and a durable recovery receipt is written authorizing cross-scope resumption. This is a real authorization hole: recovery is supposed to re-establish work under a lease that legitimately covers the checkpointed scope.

WIDER THAN SCOPE ALONE (high): get_lease returns the row even when the lease is EXPIRED or RELEASED (is_expired / released_at_utc are just fields, not filters). recover_from_checkpoint never inspects them. So recovery proceeds under an expired or released lease -- the recovering session is authorized to resume work it can no longer legitimately hold. Combined with the scope gap, recovery accepts (a) any-scope, (b) expired, (c) released, and (d) foreign-actor leases: there is no check that new_lease.actor_id == new_actor.canonical() either.

Checkpoint write path is sound: payload_sha256 over canonical JSON (recovery_receipt.rs:73-75), re-verified on recovery with PayloadHashMismatch (line 182-188). Tampered payload is caught. Unknown checkpoint -> CheckpointNotFound. Lineage walk reuses MT-076 lease_lineage. The HASH integrity and lineage parts are correct; the AUTHORIZATION parts are missing.

Checkpoint/receipt are two separate awaits (event append at line 233-236, row insert at 238-252) -- same crash-window class as MT-069 but lower severity: a recovery receipt is an audit record, not authority that gates truth. Replay would show RECOVERY_RECEIPT_RECORDED with no row; acceptable for an audit trail but worth a note.
evasion_probes: Constructed cross-scope recovery: checkpoint.scope_ref='document:DOC-A'; claim a fresh lease with scope_kind=Document scope_id='DOC-B'; call recover_from_checkpoint(checkpoint_id, ..., new_lease_id=DOC-B lease). No scope comparison exists -> recovery SUCCEEDS and writes a receipt. Hole confirmed by static path analysis.
Constructed expired-lease recovery: lease past expires_at_utc; get_lease still returns Some(row) (is_expired=true but unused) -> recovery SUCCEEDS. Hole confirmed.

Checked whether insert_recovery_receipt or migration 0155 enforces scope coverage: storage just binds new_lease_id (knowledge_crdt.rs:1274-1301); the swarm_checkpoints/recovery_receipts schema (0155) has no scope-coverage constraint linking lease.scope to checkpoint.scope.
Tampered checkpoint payload: caught by sha256 re-hash (PayloadHashMismatch). This evasion is correctly blocked.
evidence_checked: Cross-referenced AgentLaneLeaseRow.scope_ref() (knowledge_crdt.rs:231) which exists and could be compared but is not used in recovery

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
